description.S: Juicy marinated chicken and colorful vegetables grilled to perfection on skewers. This teriyaki-style chicken kebab recipe features tender chunks of chicken breast marinated in a flavorful blend of soy sauce, Sprite, and spices, then grilled alongside bell peppers and onions for a complete and satisfying meal.

L.S: 1/2 cup low-sodium soy sauce
1/2 cup Sprite, not diet
1/2 cup avocado or canola oil
1 teaspoon garlic powder
1 teaspoon horseradish (plain or horseradish sauce)
2 pounds boneless skinless chicken breasts, cut into 1-inch chunks
2 bell peppers, any color, cut into large chunks
1 sweet onion (or purple onion), cut into large chunks
Wooden or bamboo skewers

note.S: any color, cut into large chunks
or purple onion, cut into large chunks

L.S: Combine 1/2 cup soy sauce, 1/2 cup Sprite, 1/2 cup oil, 1 teaspoon garlic powder, and 1 teaspoon horseradish in a large zip-lock bag. Squeeze the bag gently to mix the marinade.

Cut 2 pounds chicken breasts into 1-inch chunks and add to the marinade bag. Press out excess air, seal, and refrigerate for 1-3 hours. Do not marinate longer than 3 hours or the soy sauce will make the chicken overly salty and the texture mushy.
Soak bamboo skewers in water for at least 30 minutes before grilling. Cut 2 bell peppers and 1 onion into chunks roughly the same size as the chicken (about 1-1.5 inches) so everything cooks evenly.
Remove chicken from marinade and discard the used marinade. Thread chicken and vegetables alternately onto skewers, leaving a small gap (about 1/4 inch) between pieces to allow heat circulation.

Heat grill to medium-high (around 400°F). Lightly grease grates with oil using a folded paper towel held with tongs. Place kebabs on the grill and cook for 12-15 minutes total, turning every 3-4 minutes (about 3 turns), until chicken reaches an internal temperature of 165°F.
Remove kebabs from grill and let rest for 5 minutes before serving. Serve over rice or with a side salad.
For gluten-free: use tamari instead of soy sauce.
Food safety: Always discard used marinade - do not baste with it. If you want a sauce for serving, reserve a portion before adding raw chicken.
Use long tongs and keep a spray bottle of water nearby for flare-ups - the oil in the marinade can cause them.
Oven alternative: Bake on a sheet pan at 425°F for 18-22 minutes, turning once halfway, until chicken reaches 165°F.
Marinating time of 1-3 hours is not included in the prep/cook time estimates.

reason.S: Creamy coconut rice complements the savory-sweet teriyaki marinade and soaks up extra juices.
Cool, tangy cucumbers provide a refreshing contrast to the charred, smoky kebabs.
Throw it on the grill alongside the kabobs for a simple, smoky veggie side.
Long bamboo skewers make assembly easy and keep hands safely away from grill heat.
Ensures chicken reaches a safe 165°F without overcooking and drying out.
Long-handled tongs for easy skewer turning and safe grill grate greasing.

L.S: kebabs
skewers
grilled
marinated
summer grilling
teriyaki marinade
